toleranceRationale: Ceilings were seeded from a dev-machine (Apple M4 Pro) measured p95, and per-metric headroom deliberately VARIES: millisecond-scale metrics (tree-render, guard-call) carry ~5-7x; sub-millisecond metrics (tree-validate, store, apps-api, gen-scripted) get absolute floors of 1-8ms, which works out to ~9-250x relative headroom because relative multipliers are meaningless at microsecond scales where a single scheduler blip dwarfs the signal. The gate catches order-of-magnitude regressions (accidental O(n^2), a dropped index, a renderer that stopped memoizing), not jitter. These are provisional dev-machine-derived values: once .github/workflows/perf.yml has produced a few runs on main, recalibrate each ceiling against real CI-runner p95 samples (procedure in bench/README.md). To legitimately raise a budget, open a PR editing this file with the new measured p95 and a one-line justification (what changed and why the higher cost is expected). CI-RECALIBRATION (tree-render:render-1000 → 80): render-1000 is the largest-tree gated metric and the noisiest on a shared GitHub runner — dev-machine p95 ~9ms, but CI p95 has been observed at ~52ms with a tail to ~82ms (the render itself is unchanged; this is scheduler/GC jitter on the biggest node count). 80ms absorbs the CI p95 variance while still tripping an order-of-magnitude regression (a dropped memo or O(n^2) on 1000 nodes lands far past 80ms). Local runs stay ~9ms. ENG-254 (automations-tick, agent-context): these two suites measure the dispatch/read overhead the tick and thread-listing fixes target. Dev-machine p95 after the fix is ~1-3ms for each (tick 1.2ms, emit 2.1ms, thread-list 1.9ms), but their p95 is unusually jitter-prone (repeat runs on a busy dev box reached 6-7ms with no code change), and thread-list additionally pays PGlite I/O. The ceilings below are therefore set at ~15-30x the measured p95 so a shared CI runner cannot flake them, which still trips the failure this gate exists for: losing the trigger_kind ref (back to a full app scan), reintroducing the N+1 cursor get, or dropping the stored thread title (back to loading every thread's full messages) all land far past these values at real row counts. Recalibrate from CI samples once perf.yml has run on main. CI-RECALIBRATION (tree-render:render-100 → 12): three consecutive CI runs on PR #381 measured p50 1.78ms (identical to main) with a p95 tail of 8.8-9.1ms and max ~10.4ms; main's runs show the same-magnitude tail landing on the neighboring validate+render-100 case (p95 8.74ms) — the tail is shared-runner scheduler/GC phase, not a render change. 12ms absorbs the observed CI tail while an order-of-magnitude regression on 100 nodes (dropped memo, O(n^2) walk) still lands far past it. Local p95 stays ~1ms.
